summary:
The patient has a condition that may have multiple potential causes including autoimmune disorders or hereditary factors such as HLA associations; however, among these options, White Spongy Nevis stands out due its clear demonstration of being passed down according to an autosomal dominant model based upon direct medical literature review findings from reliable sources.
The most compelling case supporting autosomal dominant transmission comes directly from studies confirming "white spongiform" lesions are linked by family history consistent with AD genetics rather
